title: MailerLite vs Substack (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare MailerLite and Substack: pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
intro: MailerLite and Substack serve different purposes within the email marketing landscape. MailerLite is a traditional email marketing platform designed for businesses seeking automation and campaign management, while Substack is a newsletter platform built for creators who want to monetize content directly. Understanding their core differences helps you choose the right tool for your goals.

comparison_table.tool_a: Automation, landing pages, surveys, e-commerce integrations
comparison_table.tool_b: Paid subscriptions, podcast hosting, built-in monetization

choose_a_if: You need advanced marketing automation and workflow capabilities
You want to build landing pages and capture leads through pop-ups
You run an e-commerce business and need product integrations
You prefer transparent, predictable per-subscriber pricing
choose_b_if: You're a writer or creator wanting to monetize newsletters directly
You need unlimited subscribers on a completely free plan
You want podcast hosting integrated with email distribution
You value simplicity and don't need complex marketing tools

faq.a: It depends on your use case. MailerLite is superior for businesses needing marketing automation, e-commerce features, and campaign management. Substack excels for independent writers wanting to monetize directly without paying a fixed fee. MailerLite has higher customer satisfaction ratings (4.6 vs 4.0 on G2), but this reflects their different audiences and capabilities.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, MailerLite or Substack?
faq.a: Substack is cheaper for creators since it's free forever with a revenue-share model (Substack takes 10% of subscription revenue). MailerLite charges $9/month minimum for paid plans, though its free tier supports 1,000 subscribers. For serious monetization, Substack's zero upfront cost makes it more attractive initially.

faq.q: Can I switch from Substack to MailerLite?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your subscriber list from Substack and import it into MailerLite. However, you'll lose Substack-specific features like paid subscriptions and audience monetization. The switch makes sense if you need marketing automation and e-commerce integration that Substack doesn't provide.
